topics.title: Neuroimaging of Traumatic Brain Injury Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on traumatic brain injury and related conditions, with emphasis on structural and functional network alterations, white matter microstructure, and symptom or cognitive outcomes. It also includes multimodal neuroimaging studies examining concussion, chronic injury, pediatric cases, and blast exposure.

keywords: psychedelic desynchronize specific human brain network
topics.title: Psychedelic Effects on Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psilocybin and related psychedelics alter functional connectivity, cortical dynamics, and large-scale network organization in the human brain. It also includes work on brain entropy, frequency shifts, and network plasticity underlying psychedelic drug action and therapy.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
advanced diffusion mri captures therapy relate brain plasticity
topics.title: Stroke Recovery and Motor Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural predictors and mechanisms of recovery after stroke, especially visuomotor and motor outcomes. It highlights connectivity changes, network reorganization, and imaging markers that relate to rehabilitation and disability.

keywords: hypoperfusion loadings for outcome prediction in acute ischemic stroke
deep learning models of acute stroke severity
topics.title: Stroke Lesion Networks and Outcome Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke lesions and structural or functional disconnection shape neurological deficits, including aphasia and cognitive impairment. It also includes predictive modeling of acute stroke severity and clinical outcomes using imaging-derived network measures and machine learning.

keywords: peritumoral diffusion properties indicate latent glioma subtypes
functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Glioma Network Alterations and Surgical Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on glioma and other brain tumors, emphasizing how peritumoral diffusion, structural connectome changes, and functional network remapping relate to tumor subtype and grade. It also highlights presurgical fMRI and connectivity markers used to predict postoperative deficits and understand tumor-related brain reorganization.

topics.title: Brain Mechanisms of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural mechanisms, biomarkers, and connectivity patterns underlying chronic pain and related pain phenotypes. It includes experimental and clinical pain conditions, with emphasis on central sensitization, fear avoidance, and multimodal fMRI findings.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation Networks in Parkinson Disease
topics.description: The cluster centers on deep brain stimulation in parkinson disease, especially stn-based interventions and how connectivity, network modulation, and stimulation programming shape clinical response. It also includes personalized and connectomic approaches for predicting outcomes and understanding functional network changes.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound Thalamotomy and Network Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy, especially Vim-targeted treatment for essential tremor, and the associated clinical motor improvement. It emphasizes longitudinal connectivity, functional mapping, and connectivity fingerprints linked to durable outcomes and network reorganization.

keywords: collaborative infrastructure around shared tool interfaces for neuroimaging
topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Workflow Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure, with emphasis on BIDS-based data organization, shared tool interfaces, and integrated analysis platforms. It also reflects broader efforts in neuroimaging datasets, workflow standardization, and network neuroscience applications.

keywords: cam longitudinal multimodal neuroimaging cognitive dataset
topics.title: Lifespan Brain Networks and Alzheimer Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain network organization and abnormal activity across the adult lifespan, with emphasis on the default mode network, neural oscillations, and anatomical contributions. It also includes clinical and consortium-based studies related to Alzheimer disease progression and multimodal neuroimaging datasets.

topics.title: Neuromodulation for Depression Treatment
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive and related brain stimulation approaches for depression, especially major depressive disorder and treatment-resistant depression. It emphasizes therapeutic efficacy, response, and clinical application of TMS-based and other neuromodulation techniques.

topics.title: OCD circuitry and treatment response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, especially frontostriatal and functional connectivity findings, brain activation, and related neural correlates. It also includes psychotherapy and CBT outcome studies focused on neural predictors of treatment response.

keywords: central neural actions of ibogaine in opioid use disorder
functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
topics.title: Craving and Reward in Substance Use Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on substance use disorders, especially alcohol, opioid, and cannabis-related craving, reward, and abstinence. It also includes neuroimaging links between functional connectivity, cues, and craving across addiction phenotypes.

topics.title: Individualized Developmental fMRI Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on precision fMRI and naturalistic paradigms for estimating individualized brain network organization and synchrony. It also spans developmental variation in neural dynamics and inter-subject responses in children, adolescents, and adults.

topics.title: EEG Synchrony and Social Differences in ASD
topics.description: This cluster focuses on EEG-based measures of neural synchrony and connectivity in autism spectrum disorder, including biomarkers, phase dynamics, and complexity. It also emphasizes social interaction contexts such as cooperation and parent-child dyads, along with clinically distinct subgroups and sensory symptoms.

keywords: long term aerobic exercise reconfigures directed resting state eeg network topology
topics.title: Exercise and Training Effects on Brain Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how exercise, cognitive training, and other non-pharmacological interventions shape brain plasticity and cognitive gains across aging and motor systems. It includes work on functional gradients, network reorganization, and structural or electrophysiological changes linked to improved cognition and motor function.

topics.title: Lifespan Autism Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on autism and autistic traits across development, emphasizing clinical variation, symptom patterns, and potential biological subtypes. It also includes longitudinal and lifespan brain-chart approaches to characterize white matter and other neural trajectories from early childhood through adolescence and beyond.

topics.title: Autism Connectivity and Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and ASD-related brain connectivity differences, including cerebello-cortical, functional, and morphological alterations across individuals and cohorts. It also highlights heterogeneity in autistic brain organization and the use of graph neural networks and fMRI-based analyses to study these patterns.

keywords: gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
functional and metabolic network signatures of
topics.title: Neurometabolic Connectivity in the Human Brain
topics.description: This cluster centers on metabolic connectomics and gradient organization in the human brain, linking metabolite relationships, cortical organization, and network signatures. The abstracts emphasize neurometabolic foundations and biomarkers, including glutamate-related connectivity and default mode network involvement.

keywords: allometric mri analysis reveal regional brain undersizing in renpenning syndrome
topics.title: Regional Brain Undersizing and Neurologic Dysfunction
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging findings in Renpenning syndrome, especially regional brain undersizing revealed by allometric MRI analysis. It also links brain structure and biomarker abnormalities to cognitive and motor dysfunction across broader neurologic and systemic conditions.

topics.title: Cortical Surface Geometry and Variability
topics.description: This cluster centers on cortical surface analysis, geometric mapping, and measures of multidimensional cortical variability across human brain datasets. It also includes work on developmental pathways and altered cortical architecture using surface-based and normative modeling approaches.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Tinnitus
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural correlates and brain alterations associated with tinnitus, including chronic and somatic subtypes. It highlights structural, neurochemical, and regional differences linked to phantom sound perception and suppression.

topics.title: Language Lateralization and Brain Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language dominance and lateralization vary across individuals, especially in relation to handedness, bilingualism, and neurological injury. It also includes studies of reorganization and connectivity changes measured with fMRI, diffusion MRI, and intraoperative mapping.

keywords: good manual atlas- spatial prior base delineation
topics.title: Locus Coeruleus MRI in Alzheimer Disease
topics.description: These studies focus on MRI methods and contrast measures for visualizing the locus coeruleus and related brainstem structures, including quantitative and multi-contrast approaches. The cluster also links locus coeruleus imaging to Alzheimer disease and atlas-based delineation.

topics.title: Stress, Metabolism, and Dynamic Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how acute stress, glucose-related interventions, and metabolic challenges alter brain function and connectivity. It highlights dynamic network reconfiguration, regional metabolic changes, and related neuroimaging findings in the context of cognitive and emotional processing.

keywords: the dieter 's dilemma attentional focus on health
topics.title: Neural control of food choice and distraction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cognitive control, attentional focus, and distraction shape food choice and willingness, especially in the context of healthy versus unhealthy foods. It also includes neural correlates and brain structural similarity findings related to food decisions and self-regulation.

topics.title: Lewy Body and Parkinsonian Brain Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinsonian and Lewy body disease, especially prodromal and early-stage cognitive and brainstem changes. It also highlights dynamic functional connectivity and MRI-based markers of degeneration and network alteration.

keywords: a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
topics.title: Multisite Harmonization in Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster focuses on methods and tools for correcting site and batch effects in multisite neuroimaging data. It emphasizes joint harmonization, assessment frameworks, and related approaches to improve harmonisation efficacy.

topics.title: Naturalistic fMRI of Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how naturalistic stimuli and movie viewing reveal organization, variability, and information flow in large-scale brain networks. It also includes work on functional alignment, dimensionality, and network timescales in relation to behavior and cortical structure.

keywords: somatotopic homunculus profiles of motor cortex
topics.title: Imaging Alterations in Degenerative Cervical Myelopathy
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging markers of degenerative cervical myelopathy, especially cortical and tract-related changes in the motor system. It also includes methods for mapping myelin and iron-related tissue properties using MRI-derived metrics and pipelines.

topics.title: MRI-Based Alzheimer Detection and Progression
topics.description: This cluster focuses on machine learning and deep learning approaches for detecting Alzheimer disease and related dementia outcomes from brain MRI and clinical neuroimaging data. It also includes work on progression prediction, robustness, and explainable AI for reliable diagnosis.

topics.title: Neural Representation and Encoding with Deep Models
topics.description: This cluster centers on comparing deep neural network representations with human fMRI and neural encoding data to study brain encoding, representational alignment, and voxel-level selectivity. It also includes benchmark-style evaluations and cross-subject or group-level decoding for generalization in vision and related tasks.

topics.title: MRI and Biomarkers in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on multiple sclerosis, emphasizing MRI-based markers of lesion burden, gray matter involvement, and white matter disconnection. It also connects imaging findings with disability, cognitive status, neurodegeneration, and serum biomarkers.

topics.title: Brain Aging, Cognition, and Multimodal Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain aging in older adults, linking structural and multimodal MRI measures with cognition, cognitive reserve, and lifestyle factors. It also includes biomarker and predictive approaches such as plasma proteomics and brain age gradient modeling.

keywords: convolutional neural network for brain age prediction
topics.title: Brain Age Estimation and Validation
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based brain age estimation and prediction, often using deep learning and normative modeling approaches. It emphasizes generalization, cross-population validation, and clinical relevance for dementia risk and other neuropsychiatric outcomes.

keywords: normative modelling of brain volume for diagnostis and prognostis in multiple sclerosis
distributional and centile calibration of dti normative models as training sample sizes increase
topics.title: Normative Brain Modeling in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative modeling approaches for brain structure and volume, especially in multiple sclerosis. It emphasizes calibration, longitudinal modeling, and identifying deviations from normative reference patterns for diagnosis and prognosis.

keywords: structural mri volumetry improve differential diagnosis
radiomic feature analysis for prediction of ftld
topics.title: Imaging and Biomarkers in FTD Diagnosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on frontotemporal dementia, especially behavioral variant FTD, and the use of imaging and fluid biomarkers to improve differential diagnosis. It emphasizes multimodal PET/MRI, MRI volumetry, radiomic analysis, and network-level reorganization and disconnection.

topics.title: Lifespan Brain Structure and Neurodevelopment
topics.description: Studies in clinical cohorts and worldwide population modelling examine how brain structure and matter volume change across the human lifespan. The cluster emphasizes neurodevelopmental trajectories, sex difference, and MRI-based growth chart approaches to characterize normative and pathological variation.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment and Psychopathology
topics.description: Studies in this cluster focus on brain development across childhood and adolescence, especially structural and functional changes during puberty and early adolescence. Many abstracts link neurodevelopmental variation to psychopathology, including ADHD and related symptom dimensions.

topics.title: White Matter Changes and Cognitive Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter hyperintensities, white matter integrity, and related brain structure changes in aging and neurodegenerative disease. It links these imaging findings to cognitive decline, cognitive impairment, and small vessel disease, often in clinical and memory clinic populations.

topics.title: Amyloid and Tau in Alzheimer Continuum
topics.description: This cluster focuses on Alzheimer's disease progression across the preclinical and continuum stages, emphasizing amyloid burden, tau pathology, and cognitive decline. It also highlights biomarker and imaging approaches, including PET, MRI, and blood biomarkers, to study disease heterogeneity and resilience.

keywords: prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories of brain development
infant laminar cortical myelination associated with behavior and environment
topics.title: Early-life exposures shaping brain development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how prenatal and early postnatal environmental factors, especially maternal mood and exposure, relate to infant and childhood brain development. It includes longitudinal MRI findings linking these influences to cortical maturation, connectivity, morphology, and later mental health risk.

topics.title: Hormonal Influences on Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how hormonal states and transitions in females relate to brain connectivity, structure, and emotion regulation networks. It also includes work on effective and intrinsic connectivity models, alongside related behavioral and sensory measures.

keywords: normative modeling of fetal cortical folding trajectories use spectral analysis of gyrification
topics.title: Fetal Cortical Folding and Normative Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on modeling fetal and early-life cortical folding patterns using MRI, atlas construction, and normative developmental analysis. It emphasizes gyrification, spatiotemporal surface atlases, and trajectories of cortical maturation across the lifespan.

keywords: maturation trajectories of functional brain network topology
topics.title: Neonatal Brain Network Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the emergence and maturation of functional brain networks in neonates, infants, and early childhood, including preterm development and developmental transitions. It highlights longitudinal and population-level patterns of network topology, connectivity, and brain organization.

topics.title: Neonatal Brain Injury and Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neonatal and preterm brain development, especially hypoxic ischaemic encephalopathy, therapeutic hypothermia, and related outcomes in early life. It also highlights structural brain alterations such as white matter microstructure, myelination, and amygdala changes assessed with fetal and neonatal MRI.

topics.title: High-Field fMRI Methods and Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-resolution and high-field fMRI approaches for mapping brain activity and cortical organization. It emphasizes acquisition methods, reliability, and signal/contrast mechanisms across tasks and network-level measurements.

keywords: inversion of normative cognitive aging signatures
individualized mapping of functional brain networks
topics.title: Aging and Individualized Brain Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how aging relates to changes in functional brain networks, connectome organization, and individualized brain mapping across the adult lifespan. It also highlights normative cognitive aging signatures, dynamic interactions, and cerebellar network organization in healthy and older adults.

keywords: hierarchical organization of sex differences in functional connectivity during youth
topics.title: Sex Differences in Functional Connectivity Across Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sex differences shape brain functional connectivity and network organization across youth and the lifespan. It also includes work on resting-state networks, hierarchical organization, and age-related or brain-age prediction approaches.

keywords: gan guided diffusion models for generating clinically meaningful multimodal neuroimaging data
generating clinically meaningful multimodal neuroimaging data
microstructure preserving diffusion mri super resolution
topics.title: Diffusion and Deep Learning for Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion-model and deep-learning approaches for MRI reconstruction, super-resolution, harmonization, and synthetic multimodal neuroimaging generation. It emphasizes clinically meaningful and efficient methods for accelerated medical imaging and cross-modality mapping.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity Imaging Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on imaging and analysis approaches for measuring cerebrovascular reactivity, perfusion, and baseline cerebrovascular physiology, often using ASL, BOLD, and multi-delay methods. It also includes post-processing and visualization software for CVR mapping in resting-state and hypercapnia paradigms.

keywords: cardiac pulsatility generates structured network specific bold dynamics
resting state fmri cerebrospinal fluid oscillations
explore brainstem autonomic and cardiorespiratory signal interaction
topics.title: Brain-Heart Coupling in Resting States
topics.description: This cluster focuses on interactions between cardiac, cardiorespiratory, and brain activity, especially how pulsatility and peripheral physiology shape BOLD and cerebrospinal fluid dynamics. It also includes resting-state effects, autonomic coupling, and related influences such as sleep and aging.

keywords: ex vivo human brainstem mri ps oct
topics.title: Ex Vivo Brain Microstructure and Histology
topics.description: This cluster centers on ex vivo human brain and brainstem imaging approaches that integrate PS-OCT, diffusion MRI, and histology for microstructural and cytoarchitectonic mapping. It emphasizes multimodal, high-resolution reconstruction and validation of brain architecture.

keywords: functional brain alterations in preclinical alzheimer 's
cross species evaluation of atomoxetine on default mode network connectivity
cross species quasi periodic patterns transferable network dynamics
topics.title: Cross-Species Brain Connectivity in Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on functional brain alterations and network connectivity across species, with emphasis on Alzheimer disease and related cross-species comparisons. It also includes longitudinal and multimodal neuroimaging approaches, along with calcium imaging in awake animals.

topics.title: Human visual cortex organization and mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping the spatial organization of human visual cortex, especially retinotopic structure, visual field coverage, and functional boundaries across striate and extrastriate regions. It also includes work on cortical geometry and reproducible localization of visual areas.

topics.title: Primate Brain Evolution and Cross-Species Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on evolutionary comparisons of primate brain structure and function, especially auditory and language-related regions across humans, apes, and macaques. It also includes cross-species mapping and alignment approaches used to study shared neural organization and conserved dynamics.

keywords: the effect of resolution on hippocampus segmentation
submillimeter population specific t1 weighted brain atlas
topics.title: MRI Segmentation and Morphometry Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based brain morphometry and segmentation, especially hippocampal and tissue delineation approaches. It also emphasizes reconstruction, synthesis, and resolution effects on the reliability and stability of quantitative neuroimaging measures.

keywords: ultra high b value diffusion mri
topics.title: MRI Microstructure and Physiological Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on advanced MRI methods for estimating tissue microstructure, improving angular resolution, and linking diffusion and BOLD measurements to physiological mechanisms. It also includes calibration, multimodal fingerprinting, and national research initiatives aimed at better characterization of white matter and vasculature.

topics.title: High-Resolution MRI for Subcortical Iron Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-field MRI approaches for quantifying iron and related tissue properties in deep brain structures, including the corpus callosum and other subcortical regions. It also includes work on synthetic anatomy and radiological applications for improved visualization and interpretation.

topics.title: Developmental Dyscalculia and Math Learning
topics.description: This cluster focuses on math development in children and adolescents, especially developmental dyscalculia, working memory, and changes in numerical brain networks. It also includes intervention and training studies examining math gains and neuroplasticity over time.

keywords: foundation models identify eeg biomarkers for alzheimer 's
topics.title: EEG Biomarkers Across Neurological and Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG and electroencephalography biomarkers for identifying and characterizing brain disorders, including Alzheimer disease, depression, frontotemporal dementia, sleep disturbance, and altered consciousness states. It also includes related work on neural avalanches and EEG-based signal organization across different conditions.

keywords: large multi center study diffusion mri protocols
multisite evaluation of dti alps index test retest and site effects in calsnic travel heads data
topics.title: Multisite diffusion MRI methods and optimization
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI techniques, protocol optimization, and multisite evaluation across diverse acquisition and analysis settings. It also includes tractography, DTI/DWI variants, and efforts to improve robustness, reproducibility, and translational use.

topics.title: Neural and Physiological Dynamics of Sleep
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain activity, connectivity, and hemodynamic-cerebrospinal fluid interactions change across sleep and sleep stages in humans. It also includes sleep features such as continuity, initiation, REM sleep, and characteristic events like K-complexes and spindles.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
task evoked tonic aperiodic eeg activity
topics.title: EEG Dynamics in Cognitive and Social Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG-based studies of how cognitive load, working memory, and executive control shape brain dynamics across rest and task states. It also includes work on emotion regulation and social interaction, especially oxytocin-related effects and imitation.

topics.title: EEG Biomarkers Across Cognition and Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG-based biomarkers and electrophysiological signatures linked to cognition, neurodevelopment, aging, and neurological or psychiatric conditions. It also includes work on normative modeling, microstates, and aperiodic/oscillatory features for classification and prediction.

topics.title: Cross-Disorder Structural Brain Patterns
topics.description: This cluster centers on ENIGMA-based voxel-based morphometry and related structural analyses across psychiatric and neuropsychiatric disorders. The abstracts emphasize shared transdiagnostic patterns, cortical thickness and gray-white matter differences, and cross-disorder comparisons.

topics.title: EEG Dynamics of Attention and Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG and MEG measures used to study attention, conscious perception, and early cognitive processing. It also includes work on brain signal complexity, microstates, and aperiodic dynamics as markers of cognitive state change.

topics.title: Trauma-Related Brain and Network Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on how childhood trauma, early life stress, and related adversity shape PTSD, depression, and broader mental health outcomes. The abstracts emphasize neurobiological, functional, and structural brain signatures, including network connectivity and white matter markers.

keywords: automated segmentation of post surgical resection cavities
topics.title: Brain MRI Segmentation and Anomaly Detection
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based brain imaging problems, especially automated lesion and anomaly segmentation across disorders such as multiple sclerosis, epilepsy, stroke, and glioma. It also includes work on generalizable models, quality control, and benchmarking for robust clinical deployment.

keywords: the effect of preemptive warnings on fabricated news perception an
long term dynamics of neural representations of natural
topics.title: Neural dynamics of social and natural stimuli
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain represents and tracks social and natural stimuli over time, including temporal structure, similarity, and long-term dynamics. It also includes work on fabricated news perception, preemptive warnings, and friendship formation within social navigation contexts.

topics.title: Low-Field MRI Registration and Reliability
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain MRI registration, segmentation, and longitudinal estimation, with emphasis on measuring reliability and reproducibility of subcortical shape and IDP changes. It also includes benchmarking and low-field portable MRI approaches across datasets and biobank resources.

keywords: audio visual reactivations facilitate musical skill retention
topics.title: Neural Bases of Musical Skill and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how musical skill, memory, and performance are supported by neural predisposition, brain specialization, and instrument-specific synchronization. It also includes developmental and longitudinal work on encoding, habit formation, and retention, often using twin and fMRI-based approaches.

keywords: bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
cortical oscillations reflect adaptive updating of internal reach
motor learning through imitation of fully visible
topics.title: Motor Learning and Action Observation Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how action observation, imitation, and motor execution shape sensorimotor and cerebellar representations, especially for grasping and hand actions. It also includes VR-based therapy and fMRI/MVPA studies examining reorganization and adaptive updating of internal movement control.

keywords: functional connectivity and genetic correlates of dimensional mood variation
topics.title: Multimodal biomarkers of depression risk
topics.description: This cluster focuses on depression-related phenotypes and their links to brain, genetic, metabolomic, proteomic, and cardiometabolic markers. It emphasizes multimodal stratification and biomarker discovery in large population datasets such as UK Biobank.

topics.title: Genetics and Brain Connectivity in Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: The cluster centers on how genetic architecture relates to cortical and connectivity differences across schizophrenia and other major psychiatric disorders. It also includes links to cognitive deficits, structural brain changes, and related neuropsychiatric risk mechanisms.

keywords: the connectome analysis for pediatric epilepsy surgery
topics.title: Connectome and Surgical Outcomes in Pediatric Epilepsy
topics.description: This cluster centers on how connectome and lesion-based analyses inform localization and prediction in pediatric epilepsy surgery, especially for temporal lobe and related structures. It also emphasizes postoperative seizure freedom, neurocognitive outcome, and language or verbal memory effects.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
the functional structural reorganization of connector hubs
topics.title: Network reorganization in temporal lobe epilepsy
topics.description: This cluster centers on temporal lobe epilepsy, especially refractory cases, and how structural and functional network abnormalities relate to surgical outcome. It highlights hippocampal and cortical abnormalities, connector hub reorganization, and altered excitation/inhibition and signal propagation dynamics.

keywords: cortical microarchitecture along major vascular territories
topics.title: Human Cortical Cytoarchitecture Atlasing
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping and parcellating human cortical microstructure using atlas resources and cytoarchitectonic methods. It emphasizes detailed brain maps, regional subdivision, and comparative anatomy across cortical areas such as Broca and other human cortical territories.

keywords: high fidelity infant brain mri segmentation
topics.title: Automated Brain Segmentation and Parcellation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated and deep-learning-based methods for brain segmentation, parcellation, and registration across diverse MRI and anatomical targets. It also includes work on specific neuroanatomical structures and evaluating segmentation accuracy and robustness.

keywords: longitudinal multimodal neuroimaging fusion for predicting working memory and attention
late early fusion graph neural networks
topics.title: Multimodal Brain Connectivity for Cognitive Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on integrating multimodal neuroimaging and brain connectivity data to predict cognition, attention, and working memory. It also includes representation learning and fusion methods for individualized connectome analysis and linked datasets.

topics.title: Autism White Matter Development Patterns
topics.description: This cluster focuses on autism-related differences in white matter development and microstructure across age groups, including childhood and adulthood. It emphasizes tractography and diffusion-based approaches to map spatial patterns and pathways such as the corpus callosum and cortico-cerebellar connections.

keywords: manifold prediction for white matter bundle parametrization
topics.title: White Matter Tractography and Tractometry
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for analyzing, parameterizing, and comparing white matter tracts across individuals and sites. It emphasizes tractography-based quantification, individualized abnormality detection, and atlas or normative-model approaches for white matter variability.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Brain Connectivity in Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain connectivity, network topology, and structural alterations associated with depression and bipolar disorder across diagnostic boundaries. It also includes work on symptom prediction, subtype identification, and related functional and microstructural brain abnormalities.

keywords: distinct dynamic processing circuits ieeg reveal neural pathway specialization
topics.title: Intracranial network specialization in auditory processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on intracranial electrophysiology studies using iEEG to examine how distinct dynamic processing circuits and effective connectivity support auditory and related cognitive functions. The abstracts emphasize network specialization across regions such as the thalamus and primary auditory cortex, including responses to degraded speech and memory or target-detection tasks.

keywords: characterizing epileptic brain dynamics beyond pairwise use high order information theory
topics.title: Epileptogenic Zone Localization in Epilepsy
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying epileptogenic zones and seizure onset zones in epilepsy, including focal and pediatric cases. It also includes approaches using SEEG, MEG, and direct stimulation to characterize epileptic brain dynamics and guide localization.

topics.title: Individualized Mapping of Brain Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on personalized functional brain mapping and network reorganization, especially in clinical settings such as brain tumor resection and pediatric single-hemisphere brains. It also includes work on predicting postoperative recovery and functional network changes in conditions like Huntington disease.

topics.title: Lesion and Network Bases of Neuropsychiatric Symptoms
topics.description: This cluster centers on multimodal lesion and network analyses used to link brain injury, connectivity, and neuroanatomical correlates to symptoms and disorder phenotypes, especially in schizophrenia and consciousness-related findings. It emphasizes convergent mapping approaches, brain integrity, and network architecture rather than a single disease mechanism.

topics.title: Schizophrenia-related brain network dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on schizophrenia and psychosis-spectrum risk, emphasizing cortico-cerebellar signatures and subtle alterations in brain network organization. The abstracts highlight dynamic functional connectivity, infraslow network dynamics, and graph-based measures of large-scale brain network change.

keywords: gradient base disruption of hippocampal cortical organization
topics.title: Neuroimaging Biomarkers in Schizophrenia Spectrum
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and functional brain alterations associated with schizophrenia, early psychosis, and related spectrum disorders. It emphasizes neuroimaging biomarkers, connectivity disruption, and volumetric or microstructural changes across multiple brain regions.

keywords: contextual functional connectomics of sex differences in cognition
topics.title: Dynamic Functional Connectivity and Brain-Behavior Links
topics.description: This cluster centers on how dynamic and higher-order functional connectivity patterns relate to cognition, behavior, and individual differences. It also highlights contextual effects such as arousal and sex differences, alongside individualized parcellation and connectome-based prediction approaches.

keywords: multivariate bayesian inversion for trial wise decoding
topics.title: fMRI Decoding and Transfer Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on fMRI-based decoding frameworks for reconstructing or classifying neural representations across trials and conditions. It emphasizes transferability, model comparison, and Bayesian inversion approaches for decoding brain responses under varying task demands.

keywords: individualized parcellation reveal dorsal attention network specificity
topics.title: Attention Networks in Naturalistic Contexts
topics.description: This cluster centers on attention, especially sustained and external attention, and its neural organization in the dorsal attention network. It also includes work on individualized parcellation, shared neural codes, and naturalistic behaviors or video experiences as contexts for studying brain function.

topics.title: Neural and physiological bases of fear and anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on how fear conditioning, threat processing, and anxiety-related states are represented in the brain and autonomic nervous system. It also includes work on social anxiety, overgeneralized fear, and resilience in relation to neural and physiological responses.

keywords: executive control salience network structure function coupling
topics.title: Multimodal Connectivity and Network Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on multimodal MRI and network-based approaches to characterize structural, functional, and effective connectivity in neurological disorders. It emphasizes coupling, decoupling, and directed information flow across brain networks, with applications including multiple sclerosis and other clinical conditions.

keywords: dynamic developmental trajectories of emotion neural modules
neural correlates of state and trait emotion regulation a large scale fmri study
topics.title: Neural Basis of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation processes are represented in neural systems, including cognitive reappraisal, acceptance-based strategies, and amygdala regulation. It also highlights developmental, transdiagnostic, and state-trait differences in emotion regulation as studied with fMRI and related neural measures.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Emotion and Uncertainty
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain represents emotion-related information, especially valence, arousal, ambiguity, and uncertainty. It highlights amygdala-linked and broader neural signatures underlying affective processing, individual differences such as alexithymia, and dynamic responses to emotional stimuli.

topics.title: Neural Network Models of Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on computational brain modeling, especially neural mass and biophysical network models used to study large-scale dynamics and inverse problems. The abstracts emphasize mechanisms underlying epileptic transitions, thalamocortical and cortico-hippocampal interactions, and anesthesia-related state changes.

topics.title: Neurocomputational Social Decision-Making
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neural and computational mechanisms shape decision-making in social, developmental, and risk-related contexts. It emphasizes belief updating, fairness, exploration-exploitation, and brain-based correlates of behavior across adolescence and social conflict.

keywords: microstructural basis of structure function coupling across human resting state networks
topics.title: Topology and structure-function coupling in brain networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on topological and network-based analyses of human brain connectivity, including persistent homology, cliques, loops, and community detection. It also emphasizes structure-function coupling and the microstructural and resting-state basis of multimodal and structural connectomes.

keywords: mapping areal network use academic literature
topics.title: Human Brain Connectivity Across Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how structural and functional connectivity relate across the human brain, with emphasis on development, lifespan change, and thalamocortical organization. It also includes approaches for mapping areal networks and deriving individual-level parcellations and effective connectivity.

keywords: spatiotemporal dynamics of visual mental imagery of simple and complex content
the neural dynamics of simplified mental representations
hippocampal cortical network in prospective time estimation
adaptive interplay of action observation and mentalizing networks during ambiguous social perception
topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Memory, Imagery, and Social Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neural systems support visual mental imagery, memory-related representation, and prospective time estimation across spatiotemporal dynamics. It also includes work on action observation and mentalizing networks during ambiguous social perception and reward processing.

topics.title: Directed Human Brain Communication and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on modeling and reconstructing directed human brain communication from structural connectivity and network topology. It emphasizes graph-based and diffusion-style frameworks for understanding long-range pathways, spatial organization, and multiscale network structure.

keywords: biophysical large scale brain circuit models
topics.title: Dynamic Connectivity and Brain Network Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for characterizing and modeling time-varying brain connectivity, especially in fMRI and resting-state dynamics. It combines effective connectivity, dynamical systems, and large-scale network models to explain and predict brain state changes.

keywords: assessment of neural correlates of language processing in multilingual speakers
task base fnirs evidence for shared reading
topics.title: fNIRS of Language Processing and Multilingualism
topics.description: This cluster focuses on fNIRS studies of language processing in multilingual and bilingual populations, including aphasia, shared reading, and semantic decision making. It also covers language-related neural adaptation and speech production across different linguistic contexts.

topics.title: Hippocampal Dynamics in Episodic Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal and related neural mechanisms underlying episodic memory encoding, consolidation, recall, and retrieval failure. It also highlights representational organization across hippocampal subregions and long-axis functional differences.

keywords: cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
domain specific domain general higher visual areas
topics.title: Neural Basis of Mental Imagery and Vision
topics.description: This cluster centers on how mental and visual imagery are represented and transformed in the human visual system, including distinctions such as aphantasia and allocentric coding. It also covers cortical network dynamics and decoding approaches used to study imagery, perception, and related visual processes.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Auditory Language Processing
topics.description: The cluster centers on how the brain supports language comprehension and perception, especially in auditory and speech contexts, including deafness and cross-modal plasticity. It also includes studies linking brain responses and neural correlates to proficiency, learning, and sign language processing.

topics.title: Language Networks in Broca's Region
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain regions and white-matter pathways supporting language, speech, and comprehension, especially within Broca's area and related semantic interfaces. The abstracts emphasize atlas-based mapping of lexical, semantic, and phonological processes across cortical and tract-level organization.
